acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Positive fixture fails for forbidden import
- [ ] Negative fixture passes for allowed import
- [ ] Tests deterministic across environments
description:
**Problem**
Lint behavior can silently change and lose coverage.
design: Add targeted fixtures or tests in lint crate proving expected catches and allowlist behavior.
title: Add regression fixtures/tests for boundary lint behavior

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Limits struct covers every constant in REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.19.
- [ ] Replication rejects oversize frames and batches at decode time.
- [ ] EventBody decoder rejects depth/map/array/byte-length violations.
- [ ] WAL append rejects records > MAX_WAL_RECORD_BYTES before write.
- [ ] Tests verify representative limits are enforced and surfaced as errors.
description:
**Problem**
The realtime plan hardcodes many safety limits (bytes, events, timeouts). Today these values are scattered or absent; different subsystems could silently diverge, which breaks the single canonical behavior the plan expects.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.19 (normative defaults + safety bounds) and §9.8 (flow control)
- Stateright model: beads_stateright_models/src/realtime_types_sketch.rs (Limits struct and defaults)

**Files:** src/core/limits.rs (new), src/core/mod.rs, src/daemon/repl/*, src/daemon/wal/*, src/git/checkpoint/import.rs, src/daemon/ipc.rs
design:
**Design**
- Define a single Limits struct in src/core/limits.rs with all normative defaults (frame sizes, WAL bounds, CBOR decode bounds, HLC drift, backpressure, checkpoint limits).
- Introduce small unit newtypes (Bytes, Events, Millis) or clearly name fields to avoid unit confusion across modules.
- Thread Limits through EventBody decode, WAL append, replication framing, gap buffers, and IPC inflight control.
- Make all limit enforcement explicit at module boundaries; failures return ErrorPayload(code="limit_exceeded") with details of which limit tripped.
- Allow overrides via config, but enforce that defaults are the plan values when no config is provided.

notes.author: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
notes.content: Implemented Limits struct + defaults, config plumbing, IPC frame limit, WAL record size guard, and note/label limits with tests. Remaining for later phases: EventBody CBOR decode limits and replication frame/batch enforcement once those modules land.

title: Cross-phase: Normative defaults constants + enforcement

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A canonical command exists to profile fast and slow test tiers
- [ ] Baseline output identifies the slowest binaries/tests and setup hotspots
- [ ] Results are easy to compare before/after later beads
description:
**Problem**
We have stale profiling artifacts in-repo, but no current, repeatable baseline for which test binaries and individual tests dominate wall time on today's harness. That makes optimization work too easy to misdirect.

**Design**
Add a reproducible profiling path built around `cargo nextest` timings and targeted Instruments/Samply traces for the slowest test binaries. Store summaries under `tmp/perf/tests-*` and document the hotspots they expose.

**Files**
- `.config/nextest.toml`
- `scripts/` (new or existing profiling scripts)
- `docs/PERF_HOTPATHS.md` or a focused test-perf doc
title: Instrument fast and slow test tiers with reproducible timing baselines
